mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) when UNRATE remains above 4.0 for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous expansion in retail sales and industrial production indicates synchronized demand and supply growth, which boosts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of synchronized expansion months.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, a rebound in the industrial production index reflects restored manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which lifts equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in >50% of recovery-phase observations, or predictive accuracy vanishes outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal strong consumer confidence and upcoming construction activity, which drives forward corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >60% of months where HOUST increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 240.0, it triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening and margin compression, which immediately suppresses equity prices and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >55% of triggered instances, or the threshold effect disappears when CPIAUCSL remains above 240.0 for multiple consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent nonfarm payroll growth above 0.5 percent and an unemployment rate below 4.0 percent indicate a tight labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of triggered months, or predictive power is fully explained by either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansion phases, elevated real GDP growth signals economic overheating that forces central banks to raise rates, which subsequently discounts future cash flows and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in >60% of late-cycle observations, or the negative correlation between GDPC1 and equity returns breaks down during expansion phases.
